summary: Solid second set with standout Cassidy and fiery Scarlet>Fire
blurb: The show features a strong first‑set opener and a highlight‑filled second set, including a loose but enjoyable Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain and a crunchy rendition of The Other One. Brent Mydland’s synth work shines, while Jerry’s raspy vocals and thin bass low end keep the overall impact from reaching greatness.
review: On October 1, 1988 at Shoreline Amphitheatre the Dead delivered a respectable evening that leans on a few bright spots rather than soaring to legendary status. The first set builds nicely with solid mid‑tempo numbers like “Cassidy,” but it’s the second set that provides most of the excitement: after an uneven “Space” the band kicks back into gear, delivering a loose yet enjoyable “Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain” segue and a gritty, synth‑driven take on “The Other One.” Brent Mydland is truly on fire all night, his keyboards adding both crunch and color, while Phil throws in some brilliant bass bombs despite the thin low‑end feel of the mix. Jerry’s guitar work remains sharp, but his vocals sound raspier than usual, which together with the muted bass prevents the show from feeling fully great. The evening closes on a warm “Morning Dew” and an upbeat encore of “One More Saturday Night,” making this a solid, if not spectacular, snapshot of the late‑‘80s Dead.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Mixed sentiment overall but two recordings are clearly positive, praising Brent’s keyboard fire and the strong second‑set jams; the other two mixed reviews note vocal and bass issues without overwhelming negativity, yielding a modest positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Clean soundboard mix that captures the full band, with detailed notes on set flow and standout songs despite some low‑end thinness.

key_highlights: Strong second‑set momentum after a flat “Space,” highlighted by Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain.
Brent Mydland’s synth work is described as "on fire" all night, especially on The Other One.
Cassidy stands out as a first‑set highlight; Morning Dew closes the show on a warm note.

band_performance.Jerry: Voice is consistently raspy/pipeish and often out of shape; occasional good guitar moments but vocal issues dominate.
band_performance.Phil: Bass feels thin in the mix; a few brilliant bombs noted during the second set.
band_performance.Brent: Receives strong praise for his synth work, described as "on fire" and providing crunchy texture on The Other One.
